nodes.html: Scrum is a lightweight framework that helps teams deliver value through iterative work, frequent inspection, and adaptation. It defines a small set of accountabilities, events, and artifacts — and leaves the details of how the work gets done to the team.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53

nodes.html: Scrum fits complex problems where requirements and solutions are uncertain and need to be discovered through delivery. It helps when a team needs a steady cadence to ship something useful, learn from it, and adjust — rather than committing up front to a fixed plan.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53
Element has sufficient color contrast of 7.32

nodes.html: A Scrum Team has three accountabilities — together they own delivering value each Sprint.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: Accountable for maximizing the value of the product by managing the Product Backlog.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: Accountable for the team's effectiveness and for establishing Scrum as defined in the Scrum Guide.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: Accountable for creating a usable Increment that meets the Definition of Done every Sprint.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53
Element has sufficient color contrast of 7.32

nodes.html: Five time-boxed events create regularity and remove the need for other meetings.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: A fixed, time-boxed container (one month or less) inside which all the other Scrum events happen and an Increment is created.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: Initiates the Sprint by defining why it is valuable, what can be done, and how the chosen work will get done.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: A 15-minute event for Developers to inspect progress toward the Sprint Goal and adapt the plan for the next day.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: Inspects the outcome of the Sprint with stakeholders and determines future adaptations to the Product Backlog.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: Closes the Sprint by planning ways to increase quality and effectiveness in how the team works together.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53
Element has sufficient color contrast of 7.32

nodes.html: Each artifact represents work or value, and each has a commitment that gives it focus.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: An ordered list of what is needed to improve the product. Its commitment is the Product Goal.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: The plan by and for the Developers — the Sprint Goal, the selected Product Backlog items, and how to deliver them.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: A concrete step toward the Product Goal. Each Increment is additive to all prior Increments and must be usable.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: The shared standard of quality an Increment must meet to be considered complete and releasable.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53

nodes.html: The Sprint is the heartbeat of Scrum. It is a fixed-length cycle, one month or less, during which the team turns ideas into a usable Increment. Sprint Planning opens it, the Daily Scrum keeps it on track, and the Sprint Review and Retrospective close it — same length, same rhythm, every time.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 7.45

nodes.html: <figcaption id="flow-caption">Roles run the Events; the Events shape and produce the Artifacts. The Sprint is the time-boxed container around all of it.</figcaption>

Element has sufficient color contrast of 7.32

nodes.html: A few common misconceptions, set straight against the Scrum Guide.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: Scrum is not a methodology with fixed scope agreed up front

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: Scrum is a lightweight framework, not a fixed methodology. The Product Backlog is ordered and refined as the team learns; changing requirements are expected, not avoided.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: The Scrum Master is not a project manager who assigns tasks

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: The Scrum Master is a true leader who serves the Scrum Team and the wider organization. Developers self-manage how the Sprint work gets done.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: The Daily Scrum is not a status meeting for managers

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.82

nodes.html: It is a 15-minute event for the Developers to inspect progress toward the Sprint Goal and adapt the plan for the next day of work.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53
Element has sufficient color contrast of 7.32

nodes.html: Short answers to questions that come up most often.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53

nodes.html: Agile is the broader mindset described by the Agile Manifesto's values and principles. Scrum is one specific lightweight framework that embodies Agile. Using Scrum well means living those values, not just running the events.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53

nodes.html: How is Scrum different from Kanban at a glance?

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53

nodes.html: Scrum uses fixed-length Sprints, three defined accountabilities, and a small set of events to inspect and adapt. Kanban focuses on visualizing flow and limiting work in progress, without prescribed roles or time-boxed iterations. Many teams blend ideas from both.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53

nodes.html: The Product Owner. They are accountable for ordering the Product Backlog and maximizing the value of the product. Developers decide how to build the selected work; stakeholders inform decisions but do not set the order.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53
Element has sufficient color contrast of 16.53

nodes.html: Yes. Scrum is intentionally lightweight and applies to any complex work where empirical process control — transparency, inspection, and adaptation — helps a team deliver value.

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 7.32

nodes.html: Read the source: The Scrum Guide on scrumguides.org

any.message: Element has sufficient color contrast of 6.17

nodes.html: Scrum is a lightweight framework that helps teams deliver value through iterative work, frequent inspection, and adaptation. It defines a small set of accountabilities, events, and artifacts — and leaves the details of how the work gets done to the team.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Scrum fits complex problems where requirements and solutions are uncertain and need to be discovered through delivery. It helps when a team needs a steady cadence to ship something useful, learn from it, and adjust — rather than committing up front to a fixed plan.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: A Scrum Team has three accountabilities — together they own delivering value each Sprint.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
Product Owner

Accountable for maximizing the value of the product by managing the Product Backlog.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Accountable for maximizing the value of the product by managing the Product Backlog.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
Scrum Master

Accountable for the team's effectiveness and for establishing Scrum as defined in the Scrum Guide.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Accountable for the team's effectiveness and for establishing Scrum as defined in the Scrum Guide.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
Developers

Accountable for creating a usable Increment that meets the Definition of Done every Sprint.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Accountable for creating a usable Increment that meets the Definition of Done every Sprint.

nodes.html: Five time-boxed events create regularity and remove the need for other meetings.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
Sprint

A fixed, time-boxed container (one month or less) inside which all the other Scrum events happen and an Increment is created.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: A fixed, time-boxed container (one month or less) inside which all the other Scrum events happen and an Increment is created.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
Sprint Planning

Initiates the Sprint by defining why it is valuable, what can be done, and how the chosen work will get done.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Initiates the Sprint by defining why it is valuable, what can be done, and how the chosen work will get done.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
Daily Scrum

A 15-minute event for Developers to inspect progress toward the Sprint Goal and adapt the plan for the next day.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: A 15-minute event for Developers to inspect progress toward the Sprint Goal and adapt the plan for the next day.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
Sprint Review

Inspects the outcome of the Sprint with stakeholders and determines future adaptations to the Product Backlog.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Inspects the outcome of the Sprint with stakeholders and determines future adaptations to the Product Backlog.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
Sprint Retrospective

Closes the Sprint by planning ways to increase quality and effectiveness in how the team works together.

nodes.html: Each artifact represents work or value, and each has a commitment that gives it focus.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
Product Backlog

An ordered list of what is needed to improve the product. Its commitment is the Product Goal.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: An ordered list of what is needed to improve the product. Its commitment is the Product Goal.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
Sprint Backlog

The plan by and for the Developers — the Sprint Goal, the selected Product Backlog items, and how to deliver them.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: The plan by and for the Developers — the Sprint Goal, the selected Product Backlog items, and how to deliver them.

nodes.html:
Increment

A concrete step toward the Product Goal. Each Increment is additive to all prior Increments and must be usable.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: A concrete step toward the Product Goal. Each Increment is additive to all prior Increments and must be usable.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
Definition of Done

The shared standard of quality an Increment must meet to be considered complete and releasable.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: The shared standard of quality an Increment must meet to be considered complete and releasable.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: The Sprint is the heartbeat of Scrum. It is a fixed-length cycle, one month or less, during which the team turns ideas into a usable Increment. Sprint Planning opens it, the Daily Scrum keeps it on track, and the Sprint Review and Retrospective close it — same length, same rhythm, every time.

nodes.html: A few common misconceptions, set straight against the Scrum Guide.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Scrum is not a methodology with fixed scope agreed up front

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Scrum is a lightweight framework, not a fixed methodology. The Product Backlog is ordered and refined as the team learns; changing requirements are expected, not avoided.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
The Scrum Master is not a project manager who assigns tasks

The Scrum Master is a true leader who serves the Scrum Team and the wider organization. Developers self-manage how the Sprint work gets done.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: The Scrum Master is not a project manager who assigns tasks

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: The Scrum Master is a true leader who serves the Scrum Team and the wider organization. Developers self-manage how the Sprint work gets done.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
The Daily Scrum is not a status meeting for managers

It is a 15-minute event for the Developers to inspect progress toward the Sprint Goal and adapt the plan for the next day of work.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: The Daily Scrum is not a status meeting for managers

nodes.html: Short answers to questions that come up most often.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Agile is the broader mindset described by the Agile Manifesto's values and principles. Scrum is one specific lightweight framework that embodies Agile. Using Scrum well means living those values, not just running the events.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: How is Scrum different from Kanban at a glance?

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Scrum uses fixed-length Sprints, three defined accountabilities, and a small set of events to inspect and adapt. Kanban focuses on visualizing flow and limiting work in progress, without prescribed roles or time-boxed iterations. Many teams blend ideas from both.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: The Product Owner. They are accountable for ordering the Product Backlog and maximizing the value of the product. Developers decide how to build the selected work; stakeholders inform decisions but do not set the order.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html:
Can Scrum be used outside software?

Yes. Scrum is intentionally lightweight and applies to any complex work where empirical process control — transparency, inspection, and adaptation — helps a team deliver value.

any.message: All page content is contained by landmarks
All page content is contained by landmarks

nodes.html: Yes. Scrum is intentionally lightweight and applies to any complex work where empirical process control — transparency, inspection, and adaptation — helps a team deliver value.
